title: THE SHARK DRONIE - 2016 New York City Drone Film Festival DRONIE Category Winner
creator: Behind the Mask (production company; uploaded on New York City Drone Film Festival's official YouTube channel as the 2016 DRONIE Category Winner)
award_or_showcase_provenance: New York City Drone Film Festival (NYCDFF) 2016 (2nd Annual) -- DRONIE Category Winner (also described in secondary coverage as 'Most Epic Dronie')
notes: SUBGENRE-DIVERSIFYING EXEMPLAR (single-take 'dronie' / short-form novelty format -- a fourth, independent Dronie-category exemplar alongside acopian-guilfoyle-washed-up-reynisfjara-dronie.json, chrisxgxc-the-last-dronie.json, and rubencaser-fpv-dronie-railway-bridge.json already in this pack, and the earliest-dated of the four) -- built to the same rigor/honesty standard as bbc-earth-aerial.json's original worked example. A candidate JSON handed to this task proposed this URL (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=943rlPNePpQ) with title/creator/platform/is_instagram claims; per this task's step-1 requirement none of it was trusted verbatim -- it was independently re-verified this session via two primary sources. (1) YouTube's own oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=943rlPNePpQ&format=json), which returned title='THE SHARK DRONIE - 2016 New York City Drone Film Festival DRONIE Category Winner', author_name='New York City Drone Film Festival', author_url='https://www.youtube.com/@NewYorkCityDroneFilmFestival' -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's title and stated channel. (2) A direct curl fetch of the raw watch-page HTML (desktop User-Agent, ~1.1MB of markup), which independently confirmed: shortDescription='THE SHARK DRONIE by BEHIND THE MASK ...' (i.e. the actual production credit, distinct from the uploading channel); lengthSeconds='79' (corroborated by three separate approxDurationMs values in the 78,812-78,901ms range, i.e. ~78.8-78.9s, consistent with a rounded 79-second runtime -- so `duration` is set to 79, a genuinely page-sourced figure rather than a guess); author='New York City Drone Film Festival'; channelId='UCKmCUpwNopeNYHy9-yuXaYA'; publishDate/uploadDate='2016-03-30T18:48:24-07:00'; category='People & Blogs'. A case-insensitive grep of that same raw page for 'licensedForReuse', 'creative commons', and a JSON '"license":' key returned zero matches anywhere in the markup -- only the standard Google/YouTube copyright footer -- so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `license_category` is set to 'all-rights-reserved'; consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, and `flagged_for_user_signoff` is false (this task's flagging rule only triggers when a cc-by/cc0/public-domain/owned license is independently verified, which is not the case here). `creator` is recorded as 'Behind the Mask' (the production company) rather than the uploading channel's own oEmbed author_name alone: the raw page's own shortDescription field ('THE SHARK DRONIE by BEHIND THE MASK') directly credits the production company, and this was independently corroborated by (a) a WebFetch this session of the festival's own winners page (https://www.nycdronefilmfestival.com/winners), which lists the 2016 Dronie-category winner as 'The Shark Drone(ie)' by 'Behind The Mask' -- a direct primary-source confirmation, matching the exact wording ('THE SHARK DRONE by Behind The Mask') that a sibling exemplar in this pack (rubencaser-fpv-dronie-railway-bridge.json, verified independently in a separate session) also recorded seeing on that same winners page; and (b) WebSearch results surfacing a Facebook post from Behind the Mask's own page captioned 'The Shark Dronie | This is the Shark Dronie ... awarded "Most Epic Dronie" at the 2016 New York City Drone Film Festival', plus a Behind the Mask company site (behind-the-mask.com) describing itself as an underwater film production and dive-travel outfit. `award_or_showcase_provenance` therefore rests on a directly-sourced, primary-source-confirmed juried-contest win (the festival's own winners page), not an inferred or elimination-based attribution, matching this pack's existing precedent for contest-verified exemplars (e.g. ny-drone-film-festival.json, acopian-guilfoyle-washed-up-reynisfjara-dronie.json). One nuance disclosed rather than silently adopted: a WebSearch-synthesized (not directly source-quoted) summary additionally attributed direction/piloting to 'Florian Fischer' (with co-pilot 'Christian Back') and a filming location of Eleuthera, Bahamas -- these production-crew and location details were not independently confirmed against a directly-quoted primary source in this session (the search engine's own synthesized-answer text, not a linked page's verbatim content, was the only place they appeared), so they are noted here for context but are NOT asserted into the `creator` or `award_or_showcase_provenance` fields themselves. `platform`='youtube' and the discovery-stage `is_instagram=false` flag are both confirmed: the source_url's host is youtube.com/watch, not an Instagram URL, so the Instagram media-extraction prohibition is not triggered (though `local_media_path` would stay null regardless, per this project's blanket no-download policy). `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint, and no live playback of this specific video was performed in this session. Calibration reasoning, benchmarked against this pack's three existing Dronie-category exemplars: sharpness=0.68 sits at the low end of this pack's range, in line with acopian-guilfoyle-washed-up-reynisfjara-dronie.json's 0.68 (a similarly early, 2016-vintage clip) and below chrisxgxc-the-last-dronie.json's 0.72 and rubencaser-fpv-dronie-railway-bridge.json's 0.73 -- no drone/camera model or resolution was confirmed anywhere in this session's research for this specific clip, and an underwater-capable rig transitioning into open air is a genuinely harder optical case (housing-glass/water-surface refraction and splash residue during the underwater-to-air transition point) than a purely aerial or purely surface-level dronie. exposure=0.64 is set BELOW all three existing Dronie exemplars in this pack (0.66/0.73/0.73): this is a deliberate, reasoned markdown, not an arbitrary number -- the shot's own defining premise (per every corroborating description found this session: 'a solitary scuba diver awash in sharks... while an underwater drone zipped up and into the sky, recording all along the way') is a single continuous, uncut exposure pass across two drastically different lighting/color-temperature regimes (dim, blue-shifted underwater ambient light near the seafloor, versus bright, unfiltered daylight sky above the surface) within one unbroken take -- a substantially more extreme dynamic-range swing, within a single continuous shot with no cut to allow an exposure reset, than any of this pack's other Dronie exemplars (a flat-overcast beach in acopian-guilfoyle, a VFX-composited scene in chrisxgxc, an FPV bridge flight in rubencaser), none of which cross a water-to-air optical boundary mid-shot. motion_smoothness=0.65 is likewise set below all three existing Dronie exemplars (0.72/0.75/0.77): a splash-through transition from underwater buoyancy to free-air flight is a physically real destabilizing event (drag/buoyancy change at the moment of surface breach) that this pack's other 'continuous take' dronies do not have to contend with, so while the clip is still credited for achieving the genre's core creative payoff (an unbroken continuous camera move, the entire premise the NYCDFF Dronie category judges), it is scored below its siblings to reflect this added, genre-atypical stabilization challenge at the water-air transition instant -- a qualitative, disclosed judgment, not a frame-level motion measurement. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-estimated: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.68, exposure=0.64, motion_smoothness=0.65, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights(weights_version='default-v1')` (equal 1/3 weighting on sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness, composition weight fixed at 0.0) via `.venv/bin/python -c "..."` in this session, returning 0.6566666666666667 (rounded to 0.6567 here) -- so the composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible, even though its three inputs are estimates rather than measurements.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: format='short-form-reel' and estimated_cut_count=0/avg_shot_length_seconds=79.0 follow from text-provenance evidence, not from having watched the clip play out: a 'dronie' is definitionally a single continuous, uncut drone-selfie take (matching this pack's identical treatment of acopian-guilfoyle-washed-up-reynisfjara-dronie.json and rubencaser-fpv-dronie-railway-bridge.json), and every corroborating description found this session (WebSearch snippets describing the diver 'sitting on the ocean floor while an underwater drone zipped up and into the sky, recording all along the way') describes one unbroken shot spanning the full runtime, not a multi-shot edit. `duration`=79 (and therefore avg_shot_length_seconds=79.0) is a genuinely page-sourced figure this session (the raw YouTube watch page's own embedded lengthSeconds/approxDurationMs fields), not an estimate -- this is a firmer basis than rubencaser-fpv-dronie-railway-bridge.json had available (that record left avg_shot_length_seconds null because no duration field was ever recovered for it). One honest caveat worth flagging, consistent with this pack's existing usa-today-sand-dune-surfing.json precedent of checking structural signals rather than trusting a title/genre label alone: this session's own oEmbed fetch returned LANDSCAPE-oriented embed dimensions (height=113/width=200, a ~16:9 ratio) for this video, and its source_url is a conventional '/watch?v=' path, not a native '/shorts/' path -- i.e. this is NOT a vertical/portrait short-form video in the technical sense that this pack's abc-news-volcano-crater-lava-fpv.json or drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json exemplars are (those had /shorts/ URLs and oEmbed-confirmed portrait dimensions). Unlike usa-today-sand-dune-surfing.json's discrepancy, though, the short-form classification here does not rest on a self-applied '#Shorts' hashtag with no other support: it rests on (1) the verified 79-second runtime itself, corroborated by (2) the video's own festival-assigned 'DRONIE' award category, an externally-documented short-form drone-selfie subgenre (WebSearch corroboration: a Time magazine piece on the 'dronies' trend explicitly defines a 'dronie' as 'a video selfie shot on a drone', and this pack's own three existing Dronie-category exemplars are treated identically). Given that duller evidentiary basis (duration + genre-award-category, not aspect ratio), 'short-form-reel' is asserted here, but the landscape-orientation discrepancy is disclosed rather than silently omitted. No live playback or scrubbing of the video was attempted in this session (browser-automation tools were available in this environment but not invoked, consistent with this pack's precedent for single-record bounded verification tasks), so `review_method` is 'text_provenance_only' and `transition_styles_observed` stays empty (no transitions would be expected in a single continuous take, and none were described in any source consulted this session).
